title: Treasury warns global businesses on sanctions risks tied to China-based teapot refineries
summary: The US Treasury issued updated guidance on April 27, 2026 warning global businesses about sanctions risks linked to China-based independent teapot refineries that allegedly process Iranian crude in violation of sanctions, as part of the broader Economic Fury enforcement push targeting Iran's oil revenue.
contents:
# Treasury Warns on China Teapot Refineries Processing Iran Crude

## Overview
The US Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) issued updated guidance on April 27, 2026 warning global businesses about sanctions risks tied to China-based independent teapot refineries that allegedly process Iranian crude in violation of sanctions. Since March 2025, OFAC has designated multiple China-based teapot refineries that have collectively processed billions of dollars' worth of Iranian-origin oil.

## Iran Revenue Picture
- Teapot refineries account for a substantial share of Iranian crude processed in China
- OFAC's Economic Fury package on April 24 designated approximately 40 shipping firms and tankers feeding the network
- US Treasury sanctioned 35 entities in Iran's shadow banking network on April 29
- 39 Iranian or Iranian-linked vessels under interdiction or restricted status since the April 13 US naval blockade

## Diplomatic Pressure Cycle
Pakistan continues to mediate Iran-US talks. EU leaders called sanctions relief premature, stalling broader nuclear negotiations. Trump publicly demanded Iran accept curbs on its nuclear program; Iran reportedly drafted a proposal to satisfy US demands and restart Strait of Hormuz oil flow under a maritime freedom coalition framework.

## Iran-Watcher Frame
Iran Observer and partner accounts treat the teapot refinery guidance as direct pressure on Beijing's complicity in sanctions evasion. Combined with maritime interdictions, banking designations, and the blockade, the late-April enforcement package squeezes Iranian oil revenue at every link of the value chain.

## Tags
iran, ofac, sanctions, china, teapot-refineries, oil-exports, maximum-pressure
